skill.name: cultural-review
skill.description: Guides reviews of code, features, and content for cultural sensitivity and Indigenous data sovereignty practices.
skill.summary: Review products and content for cultural sensitivity, consent, access control, and Indigenous data sovereignty.
skill.author: Acurioustractor

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks and weak-crypto keywords, but review found no executable code, shell commands, cryptography, network access, or prompt injection. The flagged lines are documentation checklists and ordinary prose, so the findings are dismissed as false positives.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Lines flagged as Ruby or shell backtick execution are Markdown inline code and fenced checklist blocks. They do not execute commands or instruct command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks delimit Markdown code formatting and checklist examples. No Ruby code, shell invocation, or user-controlled command path appears in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Lines flagged for weak cryptography contain description text and checklist language, not cryptographic APIs or algorithm names used in code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are prose and checklist items. No MD5, SHA-1, DES, or other weak cryptographic implementation is present.

content.user_title: Review Cultural Safety and Data Sovereignty
content.value_statement: Teams can miss consent, access, and ownership issues in culturally sensitive systems. This skill provides a structured review framework for respectful product and content decisions.
actual_capabilities: Reviews ownership, control, access, and possession concerns using an OCAP-oriented checklist.
Classifies content handling requirements across standard, medium, high, and sacred sensitivity levels.
Checks API, user interface, and database review points for consent and access safeguards.
Identifies red flags such as missing consent, broken revocation, and elder approval bypasses.
Outlines approval workflows for standard, medium, high, and sacred content.
limitations: It provides review guidance, not legal advice or cultural authority.
It does not replace consultation with affected communities, elders, or governance bodies.
It does not execute automated scans or inspect code without user-provided context.
It focuses on the documented checklist and may not cover every regional protocol.

use_cases.title: Review a Story Sharing Feature
use_cases.description: Check whether ownership, consent, revocation, and sensitivity controls are present before release.

use_cases.title: Assess Implementation Details
use_cases.description: Review API, interface, and database behavior for cultural safeguards and access restrictions.

use_cases.title: Prepare Governance Feedback
use_cases.description: Create a clear issue list for community review, elder approval workflows, and data possession choices.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Cultural Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a proposed feature.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this feature for cultural sensitivity and Indigenous data sovereignty concerns. Focus on consent, ownership, access, and revocation.

prompt_templates.title: Sensitivity Level Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Classify this content flow by sensitivity level. Explain required approvals, sharing limits, and access controls for each level.

prompt_templates.title: Implementation Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these API, UI, and database notes against the cultural review checklist. List risks, missing safeguards, and recommended changes.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Release Gate
prompt_templates.prompt: Act as a release reviewer for this culturally sensitive feature. Evaluate OCAP alignment, elder approval workflows, audit trails, and revocation behavior.

output_examples.input: A public embed option is planned for high sensitivity stories.
output_examples.output: Finding: high sensitivity stories should not allow unauthorized embedding. Recommendation: require elder approval and disable external embedding by default.

output_examples.input: A user can delete a story, but audit records keep the full original text.
output_examples.output: Finding: deletion may not meet possession expectations if full content remains. Recommendation: anonymize retained audit records and document retention rules.

output_examples.input: A sacred content workflow allows community review but has no elder approval step.
output_examples.output: Finding: the workflow misses a mandatory approval gate. Recommendation: add elder approval before access or distribution is allowed.

best_practices: Review consent, ownership, access, and revocation together, not as separate isolated checks.
Document community-specific approval rules before designing sharing or distribution features.
Treat high and sacred content as restricted by default until proper approval is recorded.
anti_patterns: Using public defaults for culturally sensitive content.
Treating elder approval as optional for sacred or restricted content.
Keeping full story content after a deletion or anonymization request.

faq.question: Does this skill provide legal advice?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured review guidance and should be paired with legal and community consultation.

faq.question: Can it replace elder or community review?
faq.answer: No. It helps prepare issues and questions, but it cannot grant cultural approval.

faq.question: What framework does it reference?
faq.answer: It references ownership, control, access, and possession principles for data governance review.

faq.question: Can it review code?
faq.answer: Yes, when users provide relevant code or design details. It checks for consent, access, revocation, and audit concerns.

faq.question: Does it handle all Indigenous protocols?
faq.answer: No. Protocols differ by community, place, and context. Use this as a checklist, not an authority.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is listed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
